ocr.text: In the context of evaluating the effectiveness of various algorithms in a specific experimental study, the accompanying data
table provides critical insights into the performance metrics associated with each algorithm. The metrics displayed, specifically
the f1 score and the treatment groups, offer a comparative analysis that is essential for understanding the algorithms' capabilities
in relation to the defined objectives of the study. With a total of 145 animals involved in the investigation, the results indicate a
significant variation in performance across different algorithms, as indicated by their respective f1 scores.
Algorithms  f1  f5  f6  f7  f8
Diagnoses  Controls  Amnestic MCI  Alzheimer's disease
Groups  Treatment  Animals
Positive control (NT)  Lucerne hay (Medicago sativa)  8 females + 8 males
Negative treated control (T)  Lucerne hay (Medicago sativa) + Albendazole drench  8 females + 8 males
Carob (CAR)  Carob pods (crushed) (Ceratonia siliqua)  8 females + 8 males
Sainfoin (S)  Sainfoin hay (Onobrychis viciifolia)  8 females + 8 males
These scores reflect the algorithms' accuracy and reliability in predicting outcomes based on the treatment administered.
Additionally, the differentiation of groups based on N values—16 and 42—highlights the diverse sample sizes utilized, which
may influence the generalizability of the findings. The mean age of the subjects, recorded at 72.3 ± 8.0 years, further
contextualizes the demographic characteristics of the subjects involved, providing a foundation for interpreting the efficacy of
the treatments in relation to age-related factors.
